papers.notes: Carrier counts are distinct patients reported in Supplemental Table S1, not alleles or families. The study states that 32 sudden-death subjects were not genotyped and were assigned their familial SCN5A mutation; Table S1 does not identify which variant-level counts include these inferred carriers, so the reported counts retain that caveat. Affected counts were assigned only where Supplemental Table S2, together with the exhaustive Table S1 count, establishes that every reported carrier had syncope, aborted cardiac arrest, or sudden cardiac death. Event-free status alone was not treated as explicitly asymptomatic or clinically normal. Outcomes are otherwise not stratified by variant, so affected and unaffected counts are null.
papers.tool_rationale: The running full text is the most complete available representation, including the SCN5A cohort composition, mutation context, and clinical outcomes. The table artifact contains no preserved variant-level rows, while OCR is limited to two figures and is unlikely to provide carrier, affected, or unaffected counts.

papers.notes: Supplemental Table 2 is duplicated in the supplied material and was counted only once. Carrier counts are taken directly from its 'Total carriers' column. The table identifies these as SCN5A variants from LQTS families but gives no variant-level clinical classification of individual carriers as affected, symptomatic, asymptomatic, or clinically normal; therefore affected and unaffected are null rather than zero. Family counts, transmission counts, and aggregate LQT3 cohort counts were not converted into person-level phenotype counts.
papers.tool_rationale: The supplemental tables provide structured variant-level rows with family, total-carrier, and maternal/paternal transmission counts. This is more suitable for SCN5A carrier evidence than the running text, which presents only aggregate LQT3 results; the figures are not necessary.

papers.notes: Only the study's 26 genotyped heterozygous infants were counted. Prior-study cohort summaries and population frequencies were excluded. The 9 non-SIDS infant-death controls were not described as asymptomatic or clinically normal, so the unaffected count is null.
papers.tool_rationale: The running full text directly reports variant-level carrier and phenotype counts, including 26 heterozygous infant hearts and SIDS versus other-cause groups. No structured tables are present, and the single image does not appear necessary to recover genotype–phenotype evidence.

variants.evidence: The study identified 17 African-American SIDS cases and 9 non-SIDS infant-death controls heterozygous for p.Ser1103Tyr, totaling 26 distinct carriers. The 17 SIDS cases were affected; the controls were deceased from other causes and were not explicitly described as asymptomatic or clinically normal.
variants.source_location: Results, first paragraph; Methods—RNA extraction, cDNA synthesis, and genotyping

papers.notes: Only the directly studied SCN5A D1790G variant is included; Delta-KPQ appears only in summaries of prior publications. The 30 treatment-study participants are a subset of the larger family cohort and were not counted again. Nine non-genotyped obligate carriers were excluded because carriers were required to be genotype-positive. Although some participants were described as asymptomatic with respect to cardiac events, they belonged to the phenotypically affected LQT3 cohort; the paper does not give a definite count of genotype-positive, phenotypically normal D1790G carriers.
papers.tool_rationale: The publisher full text contains the SCN5A D1790G carrier cohort and cardiac-event counts, whereas the table representation is only artifact metadata and does not provide variant-level person rows.

variants.evidence: Among 188 screened family members, 94 were phenotypically affected, including 85 with genotype confirmation of the D1790G SCN5A mutation and 9 non-genotyped obligate carriers. The current 30-person treatment cohort came from these families and had the mutation identified in every participant.
variants.source_location: Main text, family ascertainment and study-population paragraphs; Limitations paragraph

papers.notes: Counts come from the variant table's mutually exclusive BrS+ and BrS− patient columns; carriers are their sums. Affected is the exact BrS+ count, the variant-resolved cardiac phenotype reported by the table. BrS− carriers were not classified as unaffected because they were not explicitly asymptomatic or clinically normal; Figure 3 specifically shows BrS− p.(Arg376His) and p.(His886Gln) carriers who reached the arrhythmic endpoint. Family counts, ExAC alleles, and functional data were excluded.
papers.tool_rationale: The PMC XML artifact includes five structured tables, which are most likely to preserve variant-level SCN5A carrier and phenotype counts. The running-text preview is truncated and repetitive, while the figures concern ECG and risk-marker analyses rather than pedigree genotype evidence.

papers.notes: Counts are restricted to the registry participants. The Methods states that all 415 participants were probands diagnosed with Brugada syndrome (BrS), and the Results identifies 55 SCN5A variants in 60 SCN5A-positive probands. Table 2 states that parenthetical numbers are numbers of patients; therefore, unparenthesized variants represent one proband. All listed carriers were affected with ECG-defined BrS, including those described as asymptomatic at baseline (which meant no prior syncope or aborted cardiac arrest, not clinically normal). No genotyped relatives were included. Follow-up events, families, and functional or prediction data were not converted into carrier counts.
papers.tool_rationale: Text is the only available representation and contains the original research article’s cohort, genotype, phenotype, and follow-up content; no table, PDF, or OCR representation is supplied.

papers.notes: The mutation table's 'Number of patients' was treated as the count of distinct genotype-positive people. Disjoint rows were combined when a variant also appeared in a compound genotype (M369K, R1193Q, and G1743E). The named genotype rows account for 42 distinct patients; 16 additional SCN5A-positive patients appear under 'variant notation unavailable' and therefore cannot be represented as specific variants. The phenotype table reports only an aggregate split of SCN5A-positive patients—10 symptomatic and 48 asymptomatic—without linking status to individual variants, so affected and unaffected are null for every variant. c. and p. prefixes were added for clarity; changes otherwise follow the table's notation.

papers.notes: Only one human SCN5A variant is reported. Counts use the 53 molecularly mutation-positive family members; the 43 adult carriers in the ECG analysis are a subset and were not added again. Nine deceased obligate carriers were not added because they were pedigree-inferred rather than reported as molecularly genotype-positive. Unrelated control alleles were excluded.
papers.tool_rationale: The running full text is the most complete available representation and includes the clinical/genetic methods and family-level evidence. The table representation contains only figure metadata and captions, with no structured variant-level person counts or pedigree data.

variants.evidence: SSCP identified the exon 28 aberrant conformer in 53 affected family members, including children; sequencing demonstrated a heterozygous TGA insertion at nucleotide 5537 causing 1795insD. The conformer was absent from unaffected family members.

papers.notes: The relevant phenotype was ventricular fibrillation during acute myocardial infarction (AMI/VF+). Carrier totals include non-reference genotypes in the AMI/VF+ and AMI/VF− patient groups but exclude the control column. AMI/VF− patients were not classified as unaffected because they had acute myocardial infarction and were not explicitly asymptomatic or clinically normal; therefore unaffected counts are null. Homozygotes were counted once per person. Where genotype totals were smaller than cohort headers, only the reported genotype counts were used. Table IV identifies 630G>A, 3183G>A, and 4509C>T in AMI/VF+ patients but provides no person-level counts, so their counts are null. The paper inconsistently labels 3578G>A as R1193Q in Table III and Arg1193Glu in Table IV; the nucleotide designation is retained.
papers.tool_rationale: Structured article tables are preserved and are the clearest source for SCN5A variant-level cohort and genotype counts; the text preview is noisy and duplicated, while the available PDF and OCR concern an unrelated library form.

papers.notes: No human KCNH2 variant or carrier/patient phenotype evidence was present in the supplied OCR material. The main article body was unavailable, and Supplemental File 1 contained only publisher author-rights information (pages 1–2); therefore no variants or person counts could be extracted.
papers.tool_rationale: The text has no usable article body, while the table and PDF previews contain only unrelated journal materials. The available figure images are therefore the only representation that may preserve genotype, pedigree, and variant-level carrier/phenotype evidence.

papers.notes: Counts use the present Health 2000 cohort, which provides exact current-study genotype totals. Prior or unpublished clinical-series counts in the Discussion (including L552S n=90, earlier R176W n=30, updated R176W n=112, and compound carriers) were not added because their overlap with one another or the current cohort cannot be resolved. The 3/317 blood-donor observation was excluded as a population/control frequency. Aggregate QT differences do not establish how many individual carriers were affected or clinically normal, and symptom history was not systematically ascertained.
papers.tool_rationale: The full text directly reports variant-level evidence for KCNH2 R176W, including 16 carriers and the associated adjusted QT prolongation. The table representation contains only artifact metadata with no preserved rows, and OCR is not needed for the genotype–phenotype counts.

variants.evidence: Two study participants were heterozygous L552S carriers. Their manually measured QTc was reported only as an aggregate, 456±12 ms, so individual affected or unaffected counts cannot be determined.
variants.source_location: Results, second paragraph; Discussion, second paragraph

variants.evidence: Sixteen study participants carried R176W and, as a group, had a 22.2 ms longer adjusted QT interval than non-carriers. The paper states that personal or family arrhythmia symptoms were not systematically ascertained, so individual affected and unaffected counts are indeterminate.
variants.source_location: Results, second and fourth paragraphs

papers.notes: Extraction is limited to the selected KCNH2 table in Supplemental File 1; no usable article body was supplied. For each variant, the table's explicit 'No. of patients' was used for both carriers and affected because these are variant-positive patients in the LQT2/KCNH2 mutation summary. The table does not identify asymptomatic or clinically normal carriers, so unaffected is null rather than 0. Asterisks are table annotations and were omitted from variant names.
papers.tool_rationale: The supplement’s structured mutation tables are the clearest available source for variant-level patient counts; the running text has no usable article body and only repeats truncated table content.

papers.notes: The supplied text states that 102 genotype-positive people carried one of five different HERG/KCNH2 mutations, but it does not identify any mutation by nucleotide, protein, or other unambiguous variant designation. The pooled total cannot be assigned to individual variants, and no variant-specific affected or unaffected person counts are provided. Therefore, no resolvable variant rows can be extracted from the supplied material.
papers.tool_rationale: The running full text is the only available representation and includes the article body needed to capture family-level genotype and phenotype evidence for the five HERG/KCNH2 mutations; no separate table, PDF, or OCR representation is available.

papers.notes: KCNH2 is designated HERG in the paper. Included Table 3 variants whose Study field contains “This,” indicating patient evidence from the present study; variants cited only to earlier studies were excluded. The cohort consisted of unrelated LQTS probands, and the Results states that HERG mutations were identified in LQTS individuals. However, Table 3 reports families rather than genotype-positive people, so family numbers were not converted into carrier or affected-person counts. Variant-specific symptom status and clinically normal carrier counts are not reported; Table 1 provides only aggregate HERG symptom data. Therefore all person counts are null. Obvious OCR renderings of splice-site notation were normalized to “+1,” and dup1234–250 was rendered as dup123–250 based on the table’s A83fs/37 coding effect.
papers.tool_rationale: The reharvested full text is substantially complete and preserves the study narrative and embedded mutation tables. The parsed table artifact contains only limited HTML text and figure metadata, while OCR covers mutation-topology figures rather than variant-level carrier, affected, or unaffected counts.

papers.notes: HERG is KCNH2. For the eight LQT2 mutations, Table 1 reports 45 carriers and 16 symptomatic carriers in total. Per-variant unaffected counts are the carrier totals minus the exhaustive 'Symptomatic patients (n)' counts; these sum to 29 nonsymptomatic carriers. Controls and noncarriers were excluded. K897T is included because the paper evaluates its association with QTc, but exact patient/family counts cannot be determined: it occurred in five screening probands and an unspecified 'several' relatives, while Table 2 separately identifies 80 K897T-allele carriers among 170 KCNQ1-Fin patients; possible overlap is not resolved. Healthy-control K897T counts were excluded.
papers.tool_rationale: Text is the only available representation and is labeled as reharvested full text; it includes the KCNH2/HERG variant survey and associated cohort information. No table, PDF, or OCR representation is available to provide better-preserved variant-level carrier or phenotype counts.

variants.evidence: K897T occurred in five LQTS screening probands and an unspecified several relatives. In the separate KCNQ1-Fin cohort, Table 2 shows 63 K897T heterozygotes and 17 T897T homozygotes (80 variant-positive patients), with genotype-specific QTc values. The paper does not establish the overlap or give an exact count for the additional family carriers.
variants.source_location: Results, 'Novel polymorphism in the HERG gene,' pp. 3-4; Table 2, p. 4

papers.notes: Only one human KCNH2 (HERG) variant with carrier phenotype evidence was reported. The paper identifies 40 heterozygotes and 2 homozygotes (42 distinct carriers). Clinical status is given for 35 heterozygotes: 10 symptomatic and 25 asymptomatic; the remaining 5 heterozygotes are not phenotypically classified. Both homozygous siblings had cardiac manifestations and are counted as affected.
papers.tool_rationale: Text is the only available representation and includes variant-level evidence for HERG/KCNH2 L552S, including two homozygous affected siblings, heterozygous parents, 38 additional carriers, and symptomatic versus asymptomatic carrier groups.

variants.evidence: The mutation was found in 40 heterozygous individuals, while the two affected daughters were homozygous. Of 35 clinically classified heterozygotes, 10 were symptomatic and 25 asymptomatic. Both homozygous siblings had markedly prolonged QTc and early ventricular arrhythmias.
variants.source_location: Results, 'Identification of the HERG-Fin mutation' and 'Clinical characteristics of the HERG-Fin mutation carriers'; Results, 'Phenotypic characteristics of the index family'

papers.notes: Per-variant counts were taken from Table 1. Unaffected counts were derived as genotype-positive subjects minus symptomatic subjects (G601S: 9−3; A614V: 25−14). The Clinical prose states 28 heterozygous patients overall, which conflicts with the Table 1 per-variant total of 34; the more specific per-variant table values were retained. N629D and other variants were excluded because they appear only in cell/functional experiments or cited prior work without human carrier phenotype evidence in this paper.
papers.tool_rationale: The complete PMC running text is available and is the clearest authoritative source for this cell-based functional study. The artifact preview contains experimental figure metadata rather than variant-level carrier, affected, or unaffected person counts, and OCR is unnecessary because the figures do not appear to contain pedigree evidence.

papers.notes: The supplied authoritative table contains only aggregate phenotype statistics by mutation type, protein region, and zygosity. It provides no KCNH2 variant identifiers or variant-specific carrier/patient data. The supporting text likewise reports only cohort-level findings. Therefore no individual KCNH2 variant can be extracted without converting aggregate cohort data into unsupported variant-level person counts.
papers.tool_rationale: The structured patient-level table is the clearest available source for linking KCNH2 variants to individual carriers and associated phenotype data. The running text is fragmented and primarily provides cohort-level summaries, while the OCR inventory does not establish that figures contain necessary genotype–phenotype counts.

papers.notes: No concrete KCNH2 sequence variant can be extracted from the supplied textual representation. The Results provide only cohort-level counts (77 LQT2 patients, 36 with a positive seizure phenotype, and 30 with a personal seizure history) and state that variant identities are in Table 2; however, only the table caption/image link—not its cells—is transcribed. These cohort totals cannot be assigned to individual variants without inference.
papers.tool_rationale: The PMC running full text is complete and directly reports KCNH2/LQT2 cohort counts and seizure phenotypes. The table preview does not expose structured variant-level rows or carrier/affected/unaffected counts, while OCR contains only aggregate figures and no necessary pedigree evidence.

papers.notes: Only the supplied material was used. Each mutation-specific n is interpreted as the number of genotype-positive participants because the n values total 492, matching the abstract's 492 LQT1 patients with 54 KCNQ1 missense mutations and the tertile totals (146, 150, and 196). Cardiac-event counts are reported only for entire tertiles, not individual variants, so variant-level affected counts cannot be determined. No variant-level counts of explicitly asymptomatic or clinically normal carriers are supplied; therefore affected and unaffected are null rather than inferred from pooled outcomes.
papers.tool_rationale: The structured mutation table directly preserves variant-level KCNQ1 carrier counts (n) for the 54 missense mutations, while accompanying rows provide cardiac-event counts by conservation tertile. This is clearer and less ambiguous than the running text for person-count curation.

papers.tool_rationale: The supplemental PDF contains the participant protocol and supplemental tables with carrier-level and phenotype-count evidence for KCNQ1 c.671C>T (p.T224M), including all 124 identified carriers and QTc classifications. variants.evidence: The study identified 124 carriers among 5,521 Amish participants, with all carrier results confirmed by Sanger sequencing. Of 88 carriers undergoing detailed follow-up, 34 (38.6%) had clinical evidence of LQTS, defined as QTc >500 ms and/or Schwartz score >3.5. No deduplicated count of explicitly asymptomatic or clinically normal carriers was provided.

papers.notes: Counts combine the individually enumerated mutation-positive subjects in the selected table with the six distinct genotype-positive SCD cases reported in the Abstract. In the table, affected means syncope/dizziness and/or basal ventricular arrhythmia (VA); unaffected means no previous symptoms and no documented positive VA, including explicitly asymptomatic subjects whose basal test was unavailable. Patient 37 had no phenotype data. The three mutation-positive relatives who died after refusing testing were not added separately because the paper does not establish them as additional to the reported six SCD cases.

The Abstract additionally reports the mutation in 6 SCD cases, all affected, giving 185 carriers and 97 affected overall.

papers.notes: The paper reports only the RYR2 P2328S founder variant. The 41 carriers categorized as having no cardiac event were not counted as unaffected because this group included carriers with exercise-test arrhythmias and was not explicitly described as asymptomatic or clinically normal.
papers.tool_rationale: The preserved structured table most clearly reports variant-carrier phenotype counts, including carriers with cardiac events (n=18) and without cardiac events (n=41), while the text provides broader cohort context.

variants.evidence: The study identified 62 mutation carriers. The clinical table classifies 18 carriers as having a cardiac event; the abstract specifies three genotyped patients with aborted cardiac arrest and 15 with exercise- or stress-triggered syncope.
variants.source_location: Main text, Abstract, Results; first preserved table, columns 'No CE n = 41' and 'CE n = 18'

papers.notes: Affected status follows the paper's CPVT definition based on symptoms or stress-induced ventricular arrhythmia; sinus bradycardia alone was not counted as affected. The four carriers too young for exercise testing were not classified as unaffected. E4076K is aggregated across both unrelated families. Familial counts are null where the paper does not state exact per-variant person totals; cohort totals and penetrance percentages were not converted into counts. Family 2 is additionally inconsistent: Table 1 reports 100% penetrance, whereas the Results describe incomplete penetrance.
papers.tool_rationale: The PDF contains the complete article and preserves the layout of the mutation and clinical tables, whereas the running text is largely abstract-only and the parsed table representation is severely fragmented. It is therefore the clearest source for variant-level carrier and phenotype evidence.

variants.evidence: The family 1 proband had exercise-induced syncope and polymorphic ventricular tachycardia. Family 1 was reported to have complete penetrance on stress testing; carriers with undetermined phenotype are shown separately and are not unaffected.

papers.notes: Only one human RYR2 variant has patient/carrier phenotype evidence. Exact person counts cannot be determined from the supplied material: the abstract reports three families, while the displayed pedigree covers only Families 1 and 2, and the eight sudden deaths are not all established as distinct genotype-positive people. Partial pedigree counts were therefore not reported.
papers.tool_rationale: Variant-level carrier, affected, and unaffected evidence is likely encoded in the family pedigree figures (g001-g003), while the text provides only aggregate statements and the table artifact contains no person-level rows.

variants.evidence: The variant was reported in three CPVT families; all affected subjects carried at least one copy, and three affected sisters were homozygous. The pedigree identifies heterozygous/homozygous carriers and CPVT-positive or CPVT-negative phenotypes for Families 1 and 2, but complete person-level evidence for the third family is not supplied.
variants.source_location: Abstract; pedigree figure panels (a)-(b), Families 1 and 2 and legend

papers.notes: The supplied text omits the contents of Table 2, although Table 2 is stated to contain all 63 unique cohort mutations. Consequently, previously reported cohort mutations not named elsewhere cannot be enumerated without unsupported reconstruction. The Supplemental Table names 37 novel amino-acid variants; V377M, Y2156C, E2183V, and G4315E are explicitly described in Results as control polymorphisms and were excluded. The remaining named sequence variants are treated as the novel putative case mutations. Exact variant-level numbers are unavailable for these variants, so counts are null except where the prose gives explicit person counts. For Y4149S, only the genotyped proband and mosaic mother were counted; the two children with sudden death were not explicitly documented as genotype-positive.
papers.tool_rationale: The running full text contains the complete cohort methods, variant findings, and clinical context. The table and PDF previews are limited mainly to a supplemental cross-species conservation table and do not preserve variant-level carrier, affected, or unaffected person counts; the figures do not appear necessary for genotype-phenotype evidence.

papers.notes: Counts were taken from unique, explicitly genotyped pedigree symbols; collapsed x2/x4/x5 sibship boxes, ungenotyped relatives, and functional experiments were excluded. Unfilled genotype-positive symbols were counted as unaffected according to the phenotype-coded pedigree.
papers.tool_rationale: The variant evidence is family-segregation based and likely encoded in a pedigree figure. Running text reports four affected homozygotes and asymptomatic heterozygous relatives but does not preserve exact individual-level carrier and unaffected counts; no structured tables are present. OCR of the figure images is therefore the best source for genotype-phenotype counts.

variants.evidence: The pedigree contains 17 distinct G3118R-positive people labeled +/− or +/+: six in generation III and eleven in generation IV. Four genotype-positive symbols are filled for cardiac arrest, and two are hatched for polymorphic PVCs/ventricular tachycardia during stress testing; the remaining 11 carrier symbols are unfilled. The abstract also describes four family cases of ventricular fibrillation and sudden death.
variants.source_location: Abstract, Methods and Results; Figure 1A pedigree and legend

papers.notes: Counts use Table 2's genotype-positive proband and symptomatic/asymptomatic carrier-relative columns. Each proband is affected because the cohort comprised 23 symptomatic probands; negative-tested and ungenotyped relatives were excluded. Recurrent variants were aggregated across separate probands without double-counting within a family. For family 19, the relative-count cells span both listed variants and were therefore applied to each variant. The c.1191G>A notation for p.Met3972Ile is retained exactly as rendered in the PDF despite its apparent internal inconsistency.
papers.tool_rationale: The PDF is the most complete authoritative article representation and preserves page-layout content, including mutation-level tables or supplementary references that the HTML/text extraction may omit. The parsed table artifact contains no actual table rows, and the OCR inventory provides no demonstrated pedigree-based genotype/phenotype evidence.

papers.notes: Six distinct RYR2 variants are described. Exact variant-level counts are reported only for N2386I and R420W. The remaining cohort totals cannot be allocated by variant from the supplied text, so those counts are null. R176Q co-occurred with T2504M in affected family 115 members and was not treated as a separate person set. Three sudden deaths in family 127 and other pedigree deaths were excluded because DNA/genotype was unavailable. papers.tool_rationale: The PMC XML artifact includes two structured tables and the full article context; these tables are most likely to preserve patient-by-patient RYR2 variants and associated phenotype evidence more reliably than running text, PDF extraction, or structural figures.
